narrative: Dear [Student's Name], I know this rejection letter stings—pouring your heart into law school dreams only to have the door close feels deeply unfair, and I'm here to walk you through why our prediction model saw your path to the bar exam as a coin flip, with a 50% chance of not passing. Picture it like a story of your journey so far: your Year 3 grades landing you in the 2nd decile at school stood out as the biggest signal, whispering that the rigors of advanced legal studies might still be a steep climb ahead. That low LSAT score of 26 echoed the same concern, suggesting the foundational analytical skills the bar demands could be a tougher hurdle for you than for others. Early on, your 1st-year decile of 2 reinforced this pattern, painting a picture of consistent challenges in keeping pace with peers right from the start. Your background as a Black male from a lower-middle-income family added layers the model weighed heavily, recognizing systemic hurdles that have historically tipped the scales toward tougher outcomes in high-stakes exams like the bar. Even your solid undergraduate GPA of 2.7 and full-time commitment couldn't fully counterbalance these, as they nudged the story further toward risk in the model's eyes. It's not that any one piece defines you—it's how they intertwined to forecast that 50-50 shot at failure, prompting us to protect spots for profiles with smoother projected paths. This isn't the end of your story; many have rewritten similar chapters by targeting those early academic dips or seeking extra bar prep. You've got resilience in your DNA—use this as fuel to build an even stronger case next time. Hang in there; brighter paths await.
